content:
3 min read
1 hour ago

--

Sometimes you just want to hand someone a message and walk away. s3cr3tm3 does exactly that, and absolutely nothing else.
There’s a peculiar tax we’ve all silently agreed to pay: to do the smallest thing online, you must first surrender an email, confirm it, pick a password you’ll forget, accept cookies, decline the newsletter, and dismiss the “rate us” popup. All of this — to send someone a five-word message you’d rather not put in a group chat.
I got tired of paying that tax. So I built s3cr3tm3 — https://s3cr3tm3.netlify.app — a free tool that does one thing: it lets you save a short secret message and share it (or delete it) from anywhere, without registering for anything.
That’s it. That’s the whole pitch. I know, revolutionary.
The problem, stated honestly
You want to send a Wi-Fi password to a guest. A door code to a contractor. A surprise-party detail to a co-conspirator. A throwaway note you don’t want sitting forever in someone’s inbox or scrollback.
Your options today:

• Text it, and let it live in plaintext history on two phones forever.

• Email it, same problem, now with corporate retention policies.

• Spin up some “secure vault” that wants your phone number for a 2FA you’ll never use again.
None of these are hard, exactly. They’re just heavier than the task deserves. A 30-second errand shouldn’t require an onboarding flow.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What s3cr3tm3 actually does
You paste a message — up to 1000 characters, which is plenty for a password, an address, or a mildly dramatic confession. You save it. You get a link/key. You hand that to whoever needs it. They open it. When you’re done, you delete it — from anywhere, no login required.
No registrations. No app install. No account to manage. Free, and intended to stay that way.
It’s deliberately boring, and boring is the feature.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

A 30-second walkthrough

• Open it. You land on a single screen with a text box. No carousel, no testimonials, no “trusted by 10,000 teams.” Just the box.

• Type your secret. Up to 1000 characters. Write the door code, the Wi-Fi password, the thing you don’t want screenshotted into eternity.

• Save it. One click. You get back a share link / key — the only thing the recipient needs.

• They open the link. The message shows up. No “create an account to view this” wall. They read it and move on.

• Delete it when done. From anywhere, with the key. Once it’s gone, it’s gone — no archive, no “are you sure you want to keep your data with us?” guilt-trip.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Who this is for
People who want to share one small secret without adopting a new platform to do it. That’s the entire target market, and I’m comfortable with that.
If you need enterprise key management, audit logs, and SOC 2 compliance — this is not that, and was never trying to be. If you need to text a friend the gate code without it living in your messages forever — this is exactly that.
Try it
It’s live, it’s free, and it asks you for nothing: https://s3cr3tm3.netlify.app
Paste a secret, share the link, delete it when you’re done. No tax. Refreshingly little to say about it — which, after the last decade of software, might be the most luxurious thing about it.
Built by a developer who just wanted to send a Wi-Fi password without signing up for anything. If you find it useful, that’s the whole reward.
